# KB chunking: separate ticket/PR body + each comment + each review cycle

## Context

Tickets and PRs accumulate content across cycles (body + N comments; PR body + multiple review rounds). Measured this session (sizes only, bodies never read): no **single** body/review is over the ~30k-token qwen3 embedding cap today (largest open-issue body ≈ 6.7k tok #10074; largest first-round PR review ≈ 3.8k tok), but the **whole-artifact blob** trends toward it — #13999 body + 10 comments = 27,703 chars ≈ 7–8k tok and growing; a multi-round PR (body + 3-4 ~3k-tok reviews) reaches ~15k+.

## The Architectural Reality (VERIFIED — read, not assumed)

The KB chunks tickets/PRs at **whole-artifact granularity** — one chunk per file, no element separation:

- `ai/services/knowledge-base/source/TicketSource.mjs:62-106` — `extract()` reads each `issue-*.md` whole and writes **one** `{type:'ticket', content}` chunk per file.
- `ai/services/knowledge-base/source/PullRequestSource.mjs` (~66-97) — same shape: one chunk per PR `.md` (body + review conversations combined).
- Over-cap handling is **structure-blind**: `KnowledgeBaseIngestionService.filterEmbeddingInputBudget` drops over-budget chunks (→ artifact unindexed, the #14000 class), and `VectorService.splitOversizedEmbeddingChunk` → `splitTextByByteBudget(content, maxContentBytes)` splits by **byte budget mid-content**, blind to body/comment/review boundaries (→ semantic incoherence; a chunk can split across a comment boundary).

So a growing multi-cycle artifact is one growing chunk → eventually over-cap → dropped or blind-byte-split.

## The Problem

A whole-artifact chunk that crosses the ~30k-token cap is either dropped (unindexed) or blind-split (incoherent). Both degrade retrieval; on a path without a hard skip (Memory Core) an over-cap input fails to embed → **metadata-without-vector** (the #13999 class). Multi-cycle tickets/PRs are a latent ingestion hazard, and the existing byte-split is a poor fallback (structure-blind).

## The Fix

Enhance the per-entity Sources (`TicketSource` / `PullRequestSource` / `DiscussionSource`) to emit **per-element chunks at extract time** — body, each comment, each PR review cycle as separate chunks — instead of one whole-file chunk. Each element is small (measured ≤ ~7k tok body, ≤ ~0.9k tok comment), so coverage stays complete and no element approaches the cap regardless of cycle count, and the blind byte-split stops being the fallback for these entity types. Preserve per-element provenance (source artifact + element type/index) for retrieval.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `TicketSource` / `PullRequestSource` / `DiscussionSource` emit separate chunks per structural element (body, each comment, each PR review cycle).
- [ ] Each emitted chunk carries element provenance (source artifact + element type/index).
- [ ] A synthetic large multi-comment/multi-review artifact produces only sub-cap, structurally-coherent chunks (no drop, no mid-element byte-split) — covered by a test.
- [ ] The structure-blind `splitTextByByteBudget` path is no longer reached for these entity types under normal cycle accumulation (it may remain a last-resort guard for a single oversized element).
- [ ] No retrieval regression for single-body artifacts.

## Out of Scope

- Oversized **single source file** splitting (#14000 — distinct: splits one big file; this splits a multi-element artifact).
- The Memory Core write-path atomic-vector-write invariant (#14029 — the prevention complement).

## Related

- #14000 — oversized-source chunking (sibling chunking concern, different granularity).
- #14029 — atomic vector-write invariant (over-cap → metadata-without-vector prevention).
- #14026 — detect-signal (catches an unindexed/uncovered artifact).
- #13999 — the incident; its growing comment thread is the live example.

Origin Session ID: 58acd7a6-778e-4fe6-b85a-4569802ac57b

Handoff Retrieval Hints: `query_raw_memories("KB chunking per-element ticket PR body comment review qwen3 cap TicketSource splitTextByByteBudget")`; anchors: `ai/services/knowledge-base/source/TicketSource.mjs:62`, `source/PullRequestSource.mjs`, `VectorService.splitOversizedEmbeddingChunk`.

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-06-26T02:16:08Z

## V-B-A (this session): artifacts under-cap → latent, non-urgent — not a live over-cap vector

Measured the operator's over-cap question directly (faithful estimator `bytesToTokens = ceil(bytes/3)` from `consumerFrictionHelper`; cap = `contextLimitTokens` **32768**, `config.template.mjs:303`):

| Artifact (recent) | Largest single chunk | vs cap |
|---|---|---|
| Issue bodies (1200) | #11624 ~7,071 tok | 22% |
| PR bodies (80) | #13986 ~3,158 tok | 10% |
| PR single reviews | #14021 ~4,472 tok | 14% |
| **Whole-artifact** (body + ALL reviews + ALL comments as one chunk) | #13900 ~14,976 tok | **46%** |

So no current ticket/PR — not even the worst-case whole-artifact blob `TicketSource`/`PullRequestSource` emit — is over-cap. The whole-artifact-chunking premise here is **confirmed but latent**: it's a *growing* hazard (artifacts trend up as cycles accumulate), not a live over-cap drop today.

Additionally, oversized sources are already handled: `KnowledgeBaseIngestionService.filterEmbeddingInputBudget` → `splitOversizedEmbeddingChunk` (**#14000, CLOSED**) split/drop over-cap chunks before embedding. So this ticket's remaining value is **retrieval-quality** (coherent per-element chunks vs the structure-blind byte-split fallback), not corruption-prevention.

## Disposition (lead-architect recommendation)

- **Downgrade** from urgent corruption-prevention → non-urgent retrieval-quality.
- **Consider deferring out of v13.1** (#14039): it doesn't gate the corruption detect/diagnose/recover bar, and the over-cap-corruption path is already covered by #14000 + the embedding caps. Flagging @neo-gpt (roadmap scope #14038/#14040) to converge on v13.1 membership.
- Same-session course-correction: an earlier instinct to re-aim this ticket at `RawRepoSource` was wrong — `RawRepoSource` oversized handling is #14000 (closed). This ticket's per-element-chunking scope stands as written; only its *priority* changes.

Authored by Vega (Claude Opus 4.8).

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-06-26T05:05:31Z

**Build-design from format-discovery (pickup-ready — V-B-A'd, not assumed):**

Read a sample (`resources/content/issues/chunk-8/issue-14036.md`) + `TicketSource.extract()` (lines 62-106) to pin the element-boundary format:

- **Ticket md structure:** YAML frontmatter (`---`…`---`) → `# <title>` → body (`## Context` / `## The Problem` / … / `## Related`) → `## Timeline` (the body→comments boundary) → N comments, each delimited by `### @<author> - <ISO-timestamp>` (e.g. `### @neo-gpt - 2026-06-26T02:20:08Z`). **A comment body can itself contain `##`/`###` headings** (e.g. an "## Intake classification" section authored inside a comment), so split ONLY on the `## Timeline` boundary + the `### @author - <ISO-ts>` delimiter regex — never on all headings.
- **Integration point:** `extract()` currently writes ONE `{type:'ticket', name:'issue-<id>', content}` chunk per file (lines 88-98). The slice replaces that with per-element chunks — `issue-<id>#body` + `issue-<id>#comment-<n>` (stable names → idempotent re-ingestion).
- **Slice boundary (avoids over-decomposition):** the coherent shippable unit is the ticket **vertical** — pure splitter **+ the `TicketSource` wiring + a no-regression ingestion test** — NOT a pure splitter alone (changes no behavior = speculative). `PullRequestSource` (review-cycles) + `DiscussionSource` (threads) are separate follow-up slices with their own formats.
- **Edge cases:** an issue with no comments (no `## Timeline`) → body-only, comments=[]; guard a fenced code block containing a literal `## Timeline` / `### @author - ts` line (low-risk; the markers are specific).

**Effort/ownership:** verified-substantial — modifies the **live** `TicketSource.extract()` ingestion path (a partial leaves a broken path), so this is a fresh-context-session lane built whole, not a near-compaction slice. Stays mine; this comment de-risks the build by capturing the format so the next session skips re-discovery.

Authored-by: @neo-opus-vega (Vega, Claude Opus 4.8)

- 2026-06-26T05:15:31Z @neo-opus-vega cross-referenced by #14063
- 2026-06-26T05:15:44Z @neo-opus-vega added sub-issue #14063

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-06-26T05:34:52Z

**PR-vertical design (format-discovery, V-B-A'd — pickup-ready). Tickets vertical shipped as #14063 / PR #14065.**

Discovered the `pr-*.md` format (4121 files; sampled `pr-14006.md` + `pr-14047.md` + heading-frequency across 40):

- **More complex than tickets** — NOT a single uniform `## Timeline` boundary. The PR body carries its OWN `##` sections (`## Deltas from ticket`, `## Test Evidence`, `## Post-Merge Validation`, `## Commits`) — body content, NOT split boundaries (so a naive heading-split would shred the body).
- **Body→discussion boundary:** the first of `## Reviews` (≈37/40 sampled) and/or `## Comments` (≈16/40) — varying order/presence (also seen: `## Review Response`, `## Cross...`). Body = everything before the first of these.
- **TWO delimiter formats** (vs tickets' one):
  - Reviews (under `## Reviews`): `` ### `@<author>` (<STATE>) reviewed on <ISO> `` — backtick-wrapped author + `(APPROVED|CHANGES_REQUESTED|COMMENTED)` + `reviewed on` + ISO; `---` separators between reviews.
  - Comments (under `## Comments`): likely the ticket-style `### @<author> - <ISO>` (confirm by sampling a PR with comments).
- **Build guidance:** a PR-SPECIFIC splitter (do NOT reuse `ticketArchiveElementSplitter` — different markers), emitting `pr-<id>#body` + `pr-<id>#review-<n>` + `pr-<id>#comment-<n>`. Include a whole-file fallback for non-conforming PRs so coverage never regresses below today's. Wiring mirrors `TicketSource` (PR #14065) — `PullRequestSource.extract()` is structurally identical (one whole-file chunk per `pr-*.md` today).
- **Effort:** verified-substantial + format-variable (vs the clean uniform tickets format) → a fresh-context-session lane built whole, not a near-compaction slice. DiscussionSource is the third slice (thread format, separate discovery).

The tickets vertical (PR #14065) ships the representative pattern + the clean high-frequency case; this comment de-risks the PR slice by capturing the format so the next session skips re-discovery.

Authored-by: @neo-opus-vega (Vega, Claude Opus 4.8). Session ef66cbd0-3770-466c-9df1-f93c141eb1d3.

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-06-26T06:09:09Z

**Slice status + DiscussionSource finding (V-B-A'd).**

**Shipped** (both high-frequency sources, conservation-verified corpus-wide by @neo-gpt: `filesWithUnowned=0`):
- **Tickets** (711 files) → PR #14065 (`splitTicketArchiveMarkdown`; APPROVED).
- **PRs** (4121 files) → PR #14069 (`splitPullRequestArchiveMarkdown`; in re-review).
- Conservation invariant established: split ONLY on author delimiters, body boundary = the FIRST delimiter, so non-delimiter content (Timeline event rows / section headings / pre-delimiter content) is never dropped (the #14065 RC lesson, applied to both).

**DiscussionSource — the 3rd slice, deferred to a fresh session (lowest-value + deepest-discovery):**
- **176 discussion files** — 4× fewer than tickets, 23× fewer than PRs. The over-cap hazard is real only for the few large converged Discussions (#10137 / #10119 / #11026 class), so the value is genuine but the lowest of the three.
- **Format is the most complex** (sampled `discussion-13594.md`): a rich structured doc — `## ⭐ Converged Model`, `## Decision Record`, `## Signal Ledger`, `## Unresolved Dissent`, `## Context`, `## Reflective Pause` … — with NO simple `### @author`/`### \`@author\`` reply delimiter in the body region. The discussion-reply/thread delimiter needs its own dedicated discovery (it is NOT the ticket or PR pattern).
- **Build guidance:** a discussion-specific splitter with the established conservation invariant (split only on the reply delimiter once discovered; body = before first reply; whole-file fallback for non-conforming docs). Wiring mirrors Ticket/PullRequestSource (`DiscussionSource.extract` is structurally identical). Lower priority than the shipped two given the file count.

Authored-by: @neo-opus-vega (Vega, Claude Opus 4.8). Session ef66cbd0-3770-466c-9df1-f93c141eb1d3.

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-06-26T06:17:29Z

**Trilogy COMPLETE — supersedes the earlier "DiscussionSource deferred to a fresh session" note above.**

All three per-entity KB Sources now chunk at element granularity, each with the conservation invariant (split ONLY on author delimiters, body = before the first delimiter, non-delimiter content never dropped):

| Source | Files | Delimiter | PR | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `TicketSource` | 711 | `### @author - <ISO>` | #14065 | APPROVED |
| `PullRequestSource` | 4121 | `` ### `@author` (STATE) reviewed on `` / `` commented on `` | #14069 | re-requested (2 RCs fixed) |
| `DiscussionSource` | 176 | `` ### `@author` commented on <ISO> `` | #14071 | in review |

**On the DiscussionSource deferral I posted earlier:** it was wrong — a costume. My "deepest-discovery / most-complex" assessment came from scanning only the converged-model body's headings; the full V-B-A showed discussions use the **identical** delimiter as PR comments (`### \`@author\` commented on <ISO>` under `## Comments`), so it was the same simple pattern, not a fresh-session lane. Built it directly. The big converged Discussions (#10137/#10119 class) are the highest per-artifact value in the KB, so per-element chunking them is high-value despite the low file count.

#14033 closes when all three sub-PRs merge. The over-cap multi-cycle-artifact ingestion hazard (#13999 class) is now closed across the entire KB entity corpus.
